question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/EntityRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: findByAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByAlias() uses a positional parameter ?1 with a byte[] type, and by sending alias values of varying lengths (e.g., 1 byte to max bytes) cause the DB to use different query plans for different input sizes, some of which are less efficient, raising average resource consumption by 30%+ over the 24-hour baseline?
